title: "Karmic refuses boot after update. Hangs at grub."
date: 2009-09-01
forum: Karmic Koala Testing and Discussion (CLOSED)

### Post by philinux on 2009-09-01
**See last post**

I'm chainloading from sda1 which has been working perfectly until now.
```
title        SDB Karmic
root (hd1,0)
chainloader +1
```

I'm getting this 

Starting up...
GRUB

There is no command prompt, cant type anything in just hangs.

Here is grub.cfg
```
#
# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE

# It is automatically generated by /usr/sbin/grub-mkconfig using templates

  insmod gfxterm

fi
```

### Post by VMC on 2009-09-01
Are you putting this into grub2 by hand:
```
title        SDB Karmic
root (hd1,0)
chainloader +1

```
I don't see a reference in your grub.cfg --unless I'm blind.

---

### Post by philinux on 2009-09-01
> **VMC said:**
> Are you putting this into grub2 by hand:
```
title        SDB Karmic
root (hd1,0)
chainloader +1

```
I don't see a reference in your grub.cfg --unless I'm blind.

SDA has Jaunty the above is in menu.lst to boot karmic. Has been worrking fine until today. Grub2 is on hard disk 2 partition 1. Hence hd1,0 from grub legacy.

 The last thing in todays updates was an update-grub. I chrooted into karmic but there are no grub updates as yet.

---

### Post by philinux on 2009-09-01
Solved.

chroot into karmic and the grub-install to sdb1

I dont think grub2 likes being installed to a partition. It must have lost grub during update-grub.

---

### Post by scaine on 2009-09-01
Happened to me on my Macmini which has a 250Mb EFI partition, a 10Gb root and a 40Gb Home.

Like you, the grub menu ignored key presses, so I couldn't use escape to run a single user shell and do updates.  Not having the first clue how to chroot into my broken install, I was stuck.

One quick re-install last night and a full update - problem solved.  Pity it took a full re-install though... :(

---

### Post by philinux on 2009-09-01
After a bit of searching chroot is remarkably easy. Either from another partition/disk or live cd

sudo mkdir /mnt/karmic
sudo mount /dev/sdb1 /mnt/karmic/    "/dev/sdb1 is my target"
sudo mount -o bind /proc /mnt/karmic/proc
sudo mount -o bind /dev /mnt/karmic/dev
sudo mount -o bind /dev/pts /mnt/karmic/dev/pts
sudo mount -o bind /sys /mnt/karmic/sys
sudo cp /etc/resolv.conf /mnt/karmic/etc/resolve.conf
sudo chroot /mnt/karmic /bin/bash

grub-install /dev/sda1                "or any other command you like."

One good link.
[http://www.ubuntu-inside.me/2009/06/howto-recover-grub2-after-windows.html](http://www.ubuntu-inside.me/2009/06/howto-recover-grub2-after-windows.html)
and this
[http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1156240&highlight=chroot](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=1156240&highlight=chroot)

---

### Post by scaine on 2009-09-03
Awesome - thanks man.  Not something I'll remember for sure, but honestly, I didn't even search for a solution thinking that chroot'ing an existing install was a really complex affair!  Now I know how straightforward it is (albeit not "simple") I'll use it in future.

Thanks for the insight.

---
